purpose: First-attempt smoke test that revealed Anthropic's TTL ordering rule. Default cache_control: {type: 'ephemeral'} resolves server-side to ttl='5m'. Claude Code injects its own session-wrap content blocks with ttl='1h'. Anthropic rejects 5m blocks that appear after 1h blocks.
anthropic_error_verbatim: API Error: 400 messages.0.content.6.cache_control.ttl: a ttl='1h' cache_control block must not come after a ttl='5m' cache_control block. Note that blocks are processed in the following order: `tools`, `system`, `messages`.

decoded.violation: 1h block must not come after 5m block

what_this_tells_us: cache_control IS being forwarded through Claude Code's stream-json input handler to Anthropic. This is the central viability question for κ Branch A, answered YES.
Claude Code's own session-wrap content uses ttl='1h'. Caller-supplied cache_control without explicit ttl resolves to 5m and breaks the ordering rule.
Caller content lands at position 6+ in the messages.0.content array — Claude Code injects ~6 system/wrap blocks before user content.
Implementation MUST hardcode ttl: '1h' on caller-supplied cache_control blocks until/unless Anthropic relaxes the ordering rule.
